headline: Pakistan's Solar Boom Powers a Nation
meta_description: Rooftop solar panels now supply one-fifth of Pakistan's electricity, transforming energy resilience after fossil fuel shortages.
summary_short: Private investment in rooftop solar has soared in Pakistan, with solar now providing one-fifth of the nation's total power supply.
body:
When energy shocks threatened Pakistan's power grid, an unexpected solution emerged from rooftops across the country. Private citizens began investing heavily in solar panels, and the impact has been remarkable—solar now accounts for approximately one-fifth of Pakistan's total national electricity supply.

For a nation of over 150 million people historically dependent on fossil fuels, this shift represents a genuine turning point. The transition shows how communities can adapt and innovate when faced with energy insecurity, building resilience through distributed renewable generation rather than waiting for centralized solutions.
